title: Keep context values referentially stable
description: Provide a memoized context value and split contexts by update frequency so unrelated consumers do not rerender on every provider render.
body: Use when a React Context provider re-renders often or its value is built inline. Do not pass a fresh object/array literal as the provider value each render; every consumer rerenders even if the slice they read is unchanged. Actions: memoize the value with useMemo keyed on its real inputs, split high-churn state (e.g. mouse position) from low-churn config into separate contexts, and verify consumers with the profiler. Validation signals include a render trace where consumers commit only when their data changes. Risk boundary: large fan-out contexts can stall the main thread; escalate to a state library if splitting is insufficient.
